title: Define Project Scope, Requirements, and Deliverables
summary: Guidance on documenting what is in‑scope, out‑of‑scope, the required deliverables, assumptions and constraints to prevent scope creep.
content: Specifics include a clear in‑scope and out‑of‑scope list, initial requirements, project boundaries, expected deliverables, assumptions, and constraints such as revision limits. This section clarifies what the team must produce to deem the project successful.
can_answer: How can I clearly identify in‑scope versus out‑of‑scope items for a project?
What should I include under assumptions and constraints in a Project Initiation Document?
examples: In‑scope: Design of 5 landing pages. Out‑of‑scope: Ongoing SEO services.
Assumption: Content will be provided by client within two weeks.
warnings: Missing out‑of‑scope items often lead to scope creep.
search_phrases: in scope out of scope list
